pattern: Because each index's decrement choices across queries are independent, the minimum prefix of queries needed is found by binary search combined with a per-index subset-sum reachability check.
hints: Whether index i can be reduced to exactly zero using the first k queries depends only on which of those queries cover i, and each covering query can be applied to i or skipped independently.
So for a fixed k, check every index separately: can some subset of its covering queries' values sum to exactly its current value? Track reachable sums with a small bitmask or boolean table.
Feasibility only improves as k grows, so binary search the smallest k where every index's exact value is a reachable subset sum of its covering queries among the first k.

edge_inputs.description: single index already zero needs no queries
single index needing the only query available
target unreachable even using every query
exact value only reachable via a specific subset, not the full sum

stress.description: small arrays with many small-range queries to exercise subset-sum feasibility
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    qcount = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(qcount):
        l = rng.randrange(0, n)
        r = rng.randrange(l, n)
        val = rng.randint(1, 10)
        queries.append([l, r, val])
    return [nums, queries]
